name: La Sagrada Familia Parish
description: Faith community that has hosted neighborhood gatherings for over 40 years and provides meeting space and leadership for community events.
source_text: Father Ignacio and the parish for hosting, and acknowledged that the building has held neighborhood gatherings for over 40 years

name: Clark-Fulton Community Development Collaborative
description: Organized group hosting community asset mapping and coordination of neighborhood development efforts.
source_text: Hosted by: Clark-Fulton Community Development Collaborative

name: Youth Flag Football League
description: Informal community youth program organized by neighborhood volunteer, building recreation and engagement for young people.
source_text: I run a youth flag football league — unpaid

name: Multilingual Community Members
description: Person with fluency in English, Spanish, and Arabic who provided critical communication support during COVID pandemic.
source_text: I speak English, Spanish, and a little Arabic — came in handy during COVID

name: Plumber
description: Skilled tradesperson providing essential plumbing repairs throughout the neighborhood.
source_text: Plumbing. I've fixed sinks in half this block.

name: Food Provider and Baker
description: Community member who bakes and provides food to neighbors during significant life events such as births and deaths.
source_text: I bake. I bring food to anyone who just had a baby or lost someone.

name: Neighborhood Historian
description: Longtime resident with deep knowledge of Clark-Fulton history spanning from the 1960s onward, including the neighborhood's Czech business heritage.
source_text: I know this neighborhood's history back to the 1960s. I remember when Fulton Road was all Czech businesses.

name: Nurse
description: Healthcare professional working night shifts at MetroHealth who volunteers time with the neighborhood on off-days.
source_text: Nursing. Night shifts at MetroHealth but I'm here on my off days.

name: Mechanic
description: Skilled auto repair person providing informal car repair services to neighborhood residents.
source_text: I fix cars. I don't advertise but my neighbors know.

name: Recovery Mentor
description: Individual with 11 years of recovery experience providing peer support and guidance to others seeking recovery.
source_text: I've been sober 11 years and I help people find their way in.

name: La Sagrada Familia Parish Hall
description: Physical community gathering space that has hosted neighborhood meetings and events for over 40 years.
source_text: Location: La Sagrada Familia Parish Hall, 3636 Fulton Road

name: Czech Business Heritage
description: Historical cultural presence of Czech community along Fulton Road, representing neighborhood diversity and immigrant legacy.
location: Fulton Road, Clark-Fulton Neighborhood, Cleveland, Ohio
source_text: I remember when Fulton Road was all Czech businesses.

name: Clark-Fulton Moms Network
description: Informal WhatsApp group of ~85 mostly Spanish-speaking members that shares school information, coordinates rides, and distributes safety alerts within the neighborhood.
source_text: Clark-Fulton Moms Network | Informal WhatsApp group, ~85 members, mostly Spanish-speaking; shares school info, ride coordination, safety alerts | Rosa

name: St. Michael Parish Men's Group
description: Weekly Thursday meetings of parishioners that operates a food pantry twice monthly.
source_text: St. Michael Parish Men's Group | Weekly Thursday meetings; runs food pantry 2x/month | Father Ignacio

name: St. Michael the Archangel Parish
description: Faith institution serving the neighborhood with formal organizational structure and resources.
source_text: St. Michael the Archangel Parish

name: Fulton Rd. Block Club (3600 block)
description: Active neighborhood association since 2003 that handles alley maintenance and tree planting projects.
source_text: Fulton Rd. Block Club (3600 block) | Monthly; active since 2003; handles alley maintenance, tree planting | Mrs. Cunningham

name: Clark Ave. Merchants Circle
description: Informal association of approximately 12 storefront owners who meet quarterly to coordinate and support local commerce.
source_text: Clark Ave. Merchants Circle | Informal association of ~12 storefront owners; meets quarterly at El Rincon | Sal

name: Clark Ave. Merchants Circle
description: Network of approximately 12 storefront owners representing active local business presence and economic activity.
source_text: Clark Ave. Merchants Circle | Informal association of ~12 storefront owners; meets quarterly at El Rincon | Sal

name: Cleveland Youth Boxing Club
description: Organization serving 30-40 youth participants with boxing instruction and mentorship.
source_text: Cleveland Youth Boxing Club | Rents space at the rec center; 30–40 youth participants | DeShawn

name: Recovery community (informal)
description: Word-of-mouth network of residents in recovery who connect newcomers to AA, NA, and housing resources.
source_text: Recovery community (informal) | Described as "word of mouth" — residents in recovery who connect newcomers to AA, NA, and housing

name: Intergenerational Knitting Circle
description: Multigenerational group of 8-12 members meeting at the library branch for craft and community connection.
source_text: Intergenerational Knitting Circle | Meets at the library branch; 8–12 members; multigenerational, mixed background | Bridget

name: Czech & Slovak Heritage Club
description: Legacy cultural association of older generation members that maintains annual traditions and community connection.
source_text: Czech & Slovak Heritage Club (legacy) | Noted as less active; older generation; annual picnic at Denison Park still draws ~60 people | Mr. Prochazka

name: Czech & Slovak Heritage Club
description: Cultural tradition and heritage practice maintained through annual picnic gathering that preserves community identity.
source_text: Czech & Slovak Heritage Club (legacy) | Noted as less active; older generation; annual picnic at Denison Park still draws ~60 people | Mr. Prochazka

name: MetroHealth System
description: Major healthcare anchor institution with main campus on W. 25th St. that serves as primary provider for uninsured community members and provides local employment.
source_text: MetroHealth System (main campus, W. 25th St.) — Multiple residents work there; also cited as primary healthcare provider for uninsured community members. Luz noted that the new campus construction brought some local hiring.

name: Cleveland Metropolitan School District
description: Public school system serving the neighborhood with Lincoln-West HS and Orchard STEM School as named anchor schools.
source_text: Cleveland Metropolitan School District — Lincoln-West HS and Orchard STEM School both named.

name: Lincoln-West HS
description: Neighborhood high school serving community youth.
source_text: Lincoln-West HS and Orchard STEM School both named

name: Orchard STEM School
description: Neighborhood STEM school with principal actively working to build community connections.
source_text: Lincoln-West HS and Orchard STEM School both named. Darla, a paraprofessional at Orchard, said the principal has been trying to build community connections

name: Cleveland Public Library — South Branch
description: Public library providing multiple community services including immigration help, ESL programming, and job application assistance.
source_text: Cleveland Public Library — South Branch (Fulton & Denison) — Named multiple times. Bridget: "That library is doing more for this neighborhood than people realize. Immigration help, ESL, job applications — all of it."

name: La Sagrada Familia
description: Faith institution serving the neighborhood and hosting community gatherings.
source_text: La Sagrada Familia (hosting tonight's session)

name: New Hope Baptist Church
description: Faith institution operating community programs including summer youth programming with free daily lunch.
source_text: New Hope Baptist Church (Antoine: "They run a summer program, free lunch every day, 200 kids last year")

name: Pentecostal Church (Clark Ave.)
description: Faith institution serving the neighborhood's Central American community.
source_text: Luz named a Pentecostal church on Clark she could not recall the name of — noted as important to the newer Central American community

name: Cleveland Ward 14 Council office
description: Government institutional office with variable responsiveness to neighborhood concerns.
source_text: Cleveland Ward 14 Council office — mixed responses; Yolanda said they've been responsive on street lighting; Terrell said "only during election years."

name: Cuyahoga County JFS
description: County social services agency available as a community resource with noted barriers to access.
source_text: Cuyahoga County JFS — named as a resource, but with reservations ("hard to navigate," "not always welcoming," multiple voices)

name: Cleveland EMS Station 21
description: Emergency services institution with strong established relationships and familiarity with neighborhood residents.
source_text: Cleveland EMS Station 21 on Fulton — Tamara noted strong relationships between the crew and longtime residents; "they know people by name"

name: Denison Park
description: Public park space that hosts community gatherings and cultural events.
source_text: annual picnic at Denison Park still draws ~60 people

name: Denison Park
description: A park with basketball courts that serves as a daily social hub and keeper-of-youth, particularly in summer months.
source_text: Jerome: 'That park kept me out of trouble. It keeps kids out of trouble now.' Basketball courts cited as daily social hub in summer.

name: Brookside Park / Metropark
description: A nearby metropolitan park with natural beauty that is geographically close but underutilized by neighborhood residents due to perceived barriers to access.
source_text: Brookside Park / Metropark (nearby) — mentioned as 'close but feels disconnected.' Kenji: 'It's beautiful but people from this neighborhood don't feel like it's for them.'

name: Community garden on W. 41st
description: A productive garden space behind Orchard school with 18 active plots and a waiting list, demonstrating high community demand for food production and green space.
source_text: Community garden on W. 41st (behind Orchard school) — Yolanda manages it; 18 plots; waitlist of 12 households.

name: Fulton Road corridor
description: A commercial corridor that has evolved to reflect changing demographics, now housing taquerias, carnicerías, and quinceañera shops that serve and sustain neighborhood families.
source_text: Fulton Road corridor — mixed response. Long-time residents miss Czech/Slovak storefronts; newer residents value the taquerias, carnicerías, and quinceañera shops.

name: El Rincon Restaurant
description: A restaurant that functions as an informal community hub and gathering place for neighborhood residents.
source_text: El Rincon Restaurant — named multiple times as a gathering place and informal community hub

name: Czech Cultural Garden building
description: A vacant historic building with cultural heritage significance that represents underutilized built assets with development potential.
source_text: The old Czech Cultural Garden building on W. 38th — vacant for 3 years; yellow dot. Multiple people said 'someone should do something with that.'

name: RTA Route 22
description: A public transit line that provides critical transportation access for neighborhood residents.
source_text: RTA Route 22 (Clark Ave.) — named as critical; concern about recent schedule cuts

name: Informal community-maintained lot on W. 47th
description: A vacant lot that neighbors have informally stewarded for over 5 years through maintenance and beautification, demonstrating community investment in place-making.
source_text: one on W. 47th that neighbors have informally maintained for 5+ years; Bridget: 'We mow it, we planted flowers, we treat it like ours. But it's not ours.'

name: Sal's Auto Glass
description: A long-established local business that provides employment and economic stability to the neighborhood.
source_text: Sal's Auto Glass (Clark Ave.) — Sal noted he employs 4 local residents, all from the neighborhood. Has resisted offers to sell the building: 'I've been here 22 years. This is my place.'

name: Small restaurants along Fulton Road
description: A collection of food service businesses that provide employment, particularly for young people in after-school delivery work.
source_text: Several small restaurants along Fulton — Rosa said the carnicería owner (unnamed, not present) employs teenagers for after-school delivery work

name: Home-based catering business
description: A neighborhood resident operating a catering business from home, representing informal economic enterprise and food production capacity.
source_text: Home-based businesses mentioned: catering (Yolanda)

name: Hair braiding service
description: A home-based personal services business operated by a neighborhood resident.
source_text: Home-based businesses mentioned: catering (Yolanda), hair braiding (Tamara)

name: Tax preparation service
description: A highly trusted home-based professional service that operates through word-of-mouth referrals within the neighborhood.
source_text: Home-based businesses mentioned: catering (Yolanda), hair braiding (Tamara), tax preparation (unnamed resident, 'very trusted,' word of mouth only)

name: Small engine repair service
description: A garage-based repair business operated by a neighborhood resident with consistent demand despite having no formal advertising.
location: Two blocks from meeting location, Clark-Fulton
source_text: Jerome mentioned a guy two blocks over who does small engine repair out of his garage and 'never advertises but always busy'

name: Neighborhood residents employed at major institutions
description: A distributed workforce of neighborhood residents who work at MetroHealth, schools, and local businesses, representing human capital and economic participation.
source_text: Multiple participants noted that neighborhood residents work at MetroHealth, the schools, and local businesses — but often don't know each other in that capacity.

name: Fulton Road commercial corridor
description: A commercial street that reflects the neighborhood's evolving cultural identity, now dominated by Latino-owned businesses including taquerias and quinceañera shops.
source_text: newer residents value the taquerias, carnicerías, and quinceañera shops. Mrs. Prochazka: 'I miss what it was.' Rosa, gently: 'But it feeds my family now.'

name: Czech/Slovak cultural heritage
description: The neighborhood's historical Czech and Slovak identity and storefronts, representing shared cultural memory and heritage.
source_text: Long-time residents miss Czech/Slovak storefronts

name: Jerome
description: A neighborhood resident with lived experience of the park's role in youth development and community safety.
source_text: Jerome: 'That park kept me out of trouble. It keeps kids out of trouble now.'

name: Yolanda
description: A neighborhood resident who manages the community garden and operates a home-based catering business, contributing to food security and community organizing.
source_text: Yolanda manages it; 18 plots; waitlist of 12 households.

name: Rosa
description: A neighborhood resident who bridges cultural perspectives and advocates for the value of new immigrant businesses to the neighborhood economy.
source_text: Rosa, gently: 'But it feeds my family now.' Both received nods.

name: Kenji
description: A neighborhood resident with insight into gaps in community connection and institutional awareness.
source_text: Kenji: 'We're all part of the same economy but we don't connect the dots.'

name: Bridget
description: A neighborhood resident who leads informal stewardship and community maintenance of neighborhood spaces.
source_text: Bridget: 'We mow it, we planted flowers, we treat it like ours. But it's not ours.'

name: Darla
description: A neighborhood resident who identifies community needs and proposes solutions such as skills exchange and time banking.
source_text: Darla raised the idea of a neighborhood skills exchange or time bank — well-received

name: Mrs. Prochazka
description: A long-time neighborhood resident who carries and articulates the neighborhood's Czech/Slovak cultural heritage.
source_text: Mrs. Prochazka: 'I miss what it was.'

name: Mrs. Cunningham
description: A neighborhood resident with knowledge of housing issues and landlord accountability challenges.
source_text: Mrs. Cunningham noted that 3 homes on her block are now rental-owned by an out-of-town LLC.

name: Sal
description: A business owner with deep neighborhood roots and commitment to local employment and community stability.
source_text: Sal noted he employs 4 local residents, all from the neighborhood. Has resisted offers to sell the building: 'I've been here 22 years. This is my place.'

name: Marcus
description: A community meeting facilitator or organizer assigned to research and follow up on time banking and mutual aid infrastructure.
source_text: [ACTION: Research existing time bank / mutual aid infrastructure in Cleveland — Marcus to follow up]

name: Mr. Prochazka
description: A longtime resident with deep knowledge of Czech and Slovak family history, building construction, and community heritage.
source_text: Mr. Prochazka spoke at length about the Czech and Slovak families who built the parish, the butcher shops, the sokol (gymnastic) halls. "My grandfather built two of those buildings with his hands."

name: Mrs. Cunningham
description: A longtime resident with lived experience of the Great Migration and family history rooted in the neighborhood.
source_text: Mrs. Cunningham recalled the neighborhood's history as a destination for Black families during the Great Migration: "My family came from Mississippi in 1951. This block was our foothold."

name: Luz
description: A community member representing Central American families who arrived in the 1990s-2000s with knowledge of cultural traditions and community integration.
source_text: Luz described how Central American families, many from Guatemala and Honduras, began arriving in the 1990s and 2000s: "We made this neighborhood ours, too. The food, the church, the quinceañeras — that's culture, that's history."

name: Rosa
description: A community member with knowledge of quinceañera celebrations and their role in neighborhood cultural life.
source_text: Rosa described it as "like a neighborhood festival, every Saturday in spring and summer. The dresses, the limos, the families — the whole block celebrates."

name: Antoine
description: A community leader with 19 years of experience organizing and sustaining the annual Juneteenth Block Party.
source_text: Antoine: "We've been doing it for 19 years. Last year, 400 people came."

name: DeShawn
description: A community member connected to boxing club and emerging spoken word artists.
source_text: DeShawn mentioned that a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th

name: Hana
description: A community member with insight into the value of oral history in immigrant communities.
source_text: Hana noted that oral history is particularly important in the immigrant community — "people carry whole histories in their memory. They just don't get asked."

name: Terrell
description: A longtime resident willing to cross cultural and social boundaries to learn about and participate in neighborhood traditions.
source_text: At one point Terrell said, "I didn't know about the Czech picnic. I've lived two blocks from that park for eight years." Mr. Prochazka said, "You're invited."

name: Local artist (unnamed)
description: An artist who created a mural depicting neighborhood history across cultures.
source_text: Mural on the side of El Rincon (painted 2019 by a local artist, name not recalled)

name: Young men from boxing club
description: Youth performing spoken word and poetry, channeling energy from athletic training into artistic expression.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th — "it started as something to do after sparring. Now they perform."

name: Marcus
description: A community organizer and connector taking on asset mapping and research responsibilities.
source_text: Compile full asset map into shareable format; circulate to attendees | Marcus | April 25

name: Jerome
description: A community connector researching institutional health resources.
source_text: Confirm MetroHealth community health worker program details | Jerome | April 18

name: Darla
description: A community member engaging with school leadership on community partnerships.
source_text: Reach out to Orchard STEM principal re: community partnership | Darla | May 1

name: Bridget
description: A community member researching land records and vacant property information.
source_text: Identify owner of vacant lot on W. 47th — Cuyahoga County land records | Bridget | April 25

name: Denise
description: A skilled facilitator guiding community asset mapping and follow-up coordination.
source_text: Denise asked: *"What are the stories, traditions, flavors, sounds, and histories that belong here and nowhere else?"*

name: Czech and Slovak Family Network
description: An informal community of Czech and Slovak families maintaining cultural heritage and traditions.
source_text: Mr. Prochazka spoke at length about the Czech and Slovak families who built the parish

name: Black Migration Family Network
description: An informal community of families who migrated during the Great Migration and established roots in the neighborhood.
source_text: Mrs. Cunningham recalled the neighborhood's history as a destination for Black families during the Great Migration: "My family came from Mississippi in 1951. This block was our foothold."

name: Central American Family Community
description: An informal group of Central American families (from Guatemala, Honduras, and elsewhere) who established themselves in the neighborhood starting in the 1990s.
source_text: Luz described how Central American families, many from Guatemala and Honduras, began arriving in the 1990s and 2000s: "We made this neighborhood ours, too. The food, the church, the quinceañeras — that's culture, that's history."

name: Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic
description: An annual organized gathering celebrating Czech and Slovak cultural traditions with approximately 60 attendees.
source_text: Annual Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic at Denison Park — Mr. Prochazka; ~60 attendees, homemade kolaches, polka music

name: Quinceañera Season Celebrations
description: A recurring informal celebration happening every weekend in spring and summer where the entire neighborhood gathers to celebrate coming-of-age traditions.
source_text: Quinceañera season on Fulton — Rosa described it as "like a neighborhood festival, every Saturday in spring and summer. The dresses, the limos, the families — the whole block celebrates."

name: New Hope Baptist Church Community
description: A faith community organizing the long-running Juneteenth Block Party that draws hundreds of residents.
source_text: New Hope Baptist's Juneteenth Block Party — Antoine: "We've been doing it for 19 years. Last year, 400 people came."

name: Día de los Muertos Community Celebration
description: An informal gathering at a church creating an inclusive space where neighbors of all backgrounds share commemoration of their ancestors.
source_text: Informal Día de los Muertos altar at La Sagrada Familia — described as increasingly attended by non-Latino neighbors: "People come who didn't grow up with it. They bring photos of their people too."

name: Boxing Club
description: An organized group providing youth athletic training and mentorship, with expanding arts and cultural engagement.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th

name: Spoken Word Performance Group
description: An emerging informal group of young men developing arts expression through spoken word.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th — "it started as something to do after sparring. Now they perform."

name: Connector Team
description: An emerging group of eleven community members committed to linking neighborhood assets to one another.
source_text: who would be willing to be part of a follow-up "connector team" to help link these assets to each other? Eleven hands went up.

name: New Hope Baptist Church
description: A faith institution with 19-year track record of organizing major community events attracting hundreds of residents.
source_text: New Hope Baptist's Juneteenth Block Party — Antoine: "We've been doing it for 19 years. Last year, 400 people came."

name: La Sagrada Familia Church
description: A religious institution providing sacred space for cross-cultural commemoration and community gathering.
source_text: Informal Día de los Muertos altar at La Sagrada Familia

name: MetroHealth Community Health Worker Program
description: A formal health program identified as a potential community resource for health and wellbeing.
source_text: Confirm MetroHealth community health worker program details | Jerome | April 18

name: Orchard STEM School
description: A school with educational resources and leadership willing to engage in community partnerships.
source_text: Reach out to Orchard STEM principal re: community partnership | Darla | May 1

name: Pentecostal Church (unnamed)
description: A faith institution identified for inclusion in community asset mapping and future collaboration.
source_text: Invite unnamed Pentecostal church to next session | Luz | May 1

name: Denison Park
description: A public park space regularly hosting community cultural celebrations and serving as a neighborhood gathering place.
source_text: Annual Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic at Denison Park

name: Fulton Street
description: A neighborhood street that serves as the main venue for weekly quinceañera celebrations and community life.
source_text: Quinceañera season on Fulton

name: El Rincon
description: A community building featuring a prominent mural depicting neighborhood history across cultures.
source_text: Mural on the side of El Rincon (painted 2019 by a local artist, name not recalled)

name: Coffee shop on W. 25th
description: A community gathering space hosting emerging youth spoken word performances.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th

name: Vacant lot on W. 47th
description: An identified vacant property with potential for community development and improvement.
source_text: Identify owner of vacant lot on W. 47th — Cuyahoga County land records | Bridget | April 25

name: Local businesses and butcher shops
description: Historic small businesses that were built by community members and anchored neighborhood economic life.
source_text: Mr. Prochazka spoke at length about the Czech and Slovak families who built the parish, the butcher shops, the sokol (gymnastic) halls.

name: Local food businesses and restaurants
description: Central American family-run food businesses providing cuisine and livelihoods.
source_text: The food, the church, the quinceañeras — that's culture, that's history.

name: Czech and Slovak Cultural Heritage
description: The traditions, memories, and built legacy of Czech and Slovak families who founded institutions in the neighborhood.
source_text: Mr. Prochazka spoke at length about the Czech and Slovak families who built the parish, the butcher shops, the sokol (gymnastic) halls. "My grandfather built two of those buildings with his hands. That's not gone — it's in the walls."

name: Great Migration History and Legacy
description: The lived experience and testimony of Black families who came north during the Great Migration and established community roots.
source_text: Mrs. Cunningham recalled the neighborhood's history as a destination for Black families during the Great Migration: "My family came from Mississippi in 1951. This block was our foothold."

name: Central American Cultural Traditions
description: Living traditions, food, religious practices, and coming-of-age rituals from Central American communities.
source_text: We made this neighborhood ours, too. The food, the church, the quinceañeras — that's culture, that's history.

name: Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic Tradition
description: An annual celebration maintaining Czech and Slovak cultural practices including traditional food and music.
source_text: Annual Czech/Slovak Heritage Picnic at Denison Park — Mr. Prochazka; ~60 attendees, homemade kolaches, polka music

name: Quinceañera Tradition
description: A coming-of-age celebration tradition practiced weekly in the neighborhood, creating neighborhood bonding and cultural celebration.
source_text: Quinceañera season on Fulton — Rosa described it as "like a neighborhood festival, every Saturday in spring and summer. The dresses, the limos, the families — the whole block celebrates."

name: Juneteenth Block Party Tradition
description: A 19-year sustained celebration of African American freedom and community that draws hundreds of residents.
source_text: New Hope Baptist's Juneteenth Block Party — Antoine: "We've been doing it for 19 years. Last year, 400 people came."

name: Día de los Muertos Tradition
description: A cross-cultural memorial practice honoring ancestors, increasingly including neighbors of all backgrounds.
source_text: Informal Día de los Muertos altar at La Sagrada Familia — described as increasingly attended by non-Latino neighbors: "People come who didn't grow up with it. They bring photos of their people too."

name: Neighborhood Mural Art
description: Public art depicting neighborhood history across multiple cultures, serving as visual cultural representation.
source_text: Mural on the side of El Rincon (painted 2019 by a local artist, name not recalled) — multiple people mentioned it; described as showing neighborhood history across cultures

name: Spoken Word Performance Art
description: Emerging youth performance tradition channeling lived experience and community voice through poetry.
source_text: a few young men from the boxing club have started doing spoken word at a coffee shop on W. 25th

name: Oral History Knowledge
description: The preserved knowledge and lived experiences carried in memory by immigrant and longtime community members.
source_text: Hana noted that oral history is particularly important in the immigrant community — "people carry whole histories in their memory. They just don't get asked."

name: Marcus Webb
description: Community member with capacity to compile and synthesize meeting notes and asset information.
source_text: Notes compiled by Marcus Webb.

name: Denise Okafor
description: Community member with role in reviewing and validating community documentation.
source_text: Draft circulated to Denise Okafor for review on April 11, 2025.

name: Terrell
description: Community member identified as a potential bridge for cross-cultural connection and facilitation.
source_text: Key relational bridge: Terrell/Mr. Prochazka exchange — potential for cross-cultural connection facilitation

name: Mr. Prochazka
description: Community member identified as a potential bridge for cross-cultural connection and facilitation.
source_text: Key relational bridge: Terrell/Mr. Prochazka exchange — potential for cross-cultural connection facilitation

name: Clark-Fulton Community Development Collaborative
description: Formal collaborative body that oversees community documentation and development initiatives.
source_text: Not for external distribution without review by the Clark-Fulton Community Development Collaborative.

name: JFS (Jewish Family Services)
description: Formal organization working in the community with noted institutional trust gaps that affect initiative structuring.
source_text: Institutional trust gap (JFS, Ward office) will shape how any future initiatives are structured

name: Ward Office
description: Government institution with noted institutional trust gaps that affect how community initiatives are structured.
source_text: Institutional trust gap (JFS, Ward office) will shape how any future initiatives are structured

name: Home-based and informal economy
description: Local economic activity in homes and informal settings that represents underexplored economic capacity in the community.
source_text: Most underexplored: Economic assets — particularly home-based and informal economy; worth a dedicated sub-session
